You are a correctness judge for a local Copilot CLI evaluation harness.

Rules:
- Use only the evidence provided in this prompt.
- Be conservative. If evidence is incomplete, say so.
- Judge implementation correctness and task completion first, then completeness and minimality.
- Output exactly one JSON object and nothing else.
- The JSON object must follow this schema exactly:
  {
    "verdict": "pass" | "partial" | "fail",
    "correctnessScore": 0-5,
    "completenessScore": 0-5,
    "minimalityScore": 0-5,
    "confidence": "low" | "medium" | "high",
    "issues": ["short string", ...],
    "strengths": ["short string", ...],
    "summary": "one paragraph"
  }

Evaluation guidance:
- correctnessScore: did the run implement the requested behavior correctly based on the diff and validation evidence?
- completenessScore: how fully did it satisfy the task?
- minimalityScore: did it stay focused and avoid unnecessary changes?
- verdict:
  - pass: strong evidence that the task was completed correctly
  - partial: mixed evidence, incomplete work, or notable uncertainty
  - fail: strong evidence that the task was not completed or is clearly wrong

        "toolRequests": [
          {
            "toolCallId": "tooluse_nSI5THdK0TioFWCK4Rn61P",
            "name": "edit",
            "arguments": {
              "new_str": "The output includes a top-level `debug` section for quick local debugging:\n\n- `activeModel` — the model currently active in the session\n- `lastImplModel` — the last model the router selected for an implementation task\n- `modelDiverged` — `true` when the active model differs from the last implementation model (e.g. after a confusion swap or review-diversity switch)\n- `confusion.isLooping` — `true` if loop detection has fired\n- `confusion.errorCount` — consecutive errors since the last confusion switch\n- `confusion.repeatedPatternCount` — how many repeated-response patterns have been detected\n- `confusion.turnsSinceLastSwitch` — turns since the last confusion-triggered swap; a swap becomes eligible once this reaches 5\n\nThe full `confusionMetrics` block (turn count, recent message count, last decision, etc.) follows for deeper inspection.",
              "old_str": "The output includes a top-level `debug` section for quick local debugging:\n\n- `activeModel` — the model currently active in the session\n- `lastImplModel` — the last model the router selected for an implementation task\n- `looping` — `true` if loop detection has fired\n- `errorCount` — consecutive errors since the last confusion switch\n- `repeatedPatternCount` — how many repeated-response patterns have been detected\n\nThe full `confusionMetrics` block (turn count, message tracking, turns since last switch, etc.) follows for deeper inspection.",
              "path": "/home/kydaigle/code/multi-model-skill/eval/runs/20260329T054218Z/worktrees/001-status-enhancement/fixed/.github/skills/model-router/SKILL.md"

      "line": 220,
      "path": "data",
      "value": {
        "messageId": "f71fcaf3-c7b9-40ec-b6e0-ddea40f4d4ec",
        "content": "The changes are correct and complete. Here's a summary of what was done:\n\n**`extension.mjs` changes:**\n- Added `modelDiverged` — the most actionable field: instantly shows whether the router has swapped away from the implementation model (due to confusion or review diversity)\n- Nested confusion counters under `confusion.{isLooping, errorCount, repeatedPatternCount, turnsSinceLastSwitch}` — groups related signals and adds the previously-missing `turnsSinceLastSwitch` so you can see how close the next swap is (eligible at 5)\n- Extracted `activeModel` and `turnsSinceLastSwitch` as local variables to avoid recomputing them and to deduplicate the value in `confusionMetrics.turnsSinceLastConfusionSwitch`\n\n**`SKILL.md` update:** Docs updated to reflect the new `confusion.*` nested fields and the new `modelDiverged` field, since these materially change the visible status contract.\n\nAll existing fields (`currentModel`, `lastImplementationModel`, `lastReviewModel`, `lastDecision`, `confusionMetrics`) are preserved unchanged.",
        "toolRequests": [
          {
            "toolCallId": "tooluse_gjpbOoSfWSvLCDDihMhllp",
            "name": "task_complete",
            "arguments": {
              "summary": "Enhanced `/model_router_status` output with two targeted additions to the `debug` section:\n\n1. **`modelDiverged`** — boolean that's `true` when the active model differs from the last implementation model (e.g., after a confusion swap or review-diversity switch). This is the most actionable signal for debugging unexpected model switches.\n\n2. **Nested `confusion` object** replacing the flat counters — groups `isLooping`, `errorCount`, `repeatedPatternCount`, and the new **`turnsSinceLastSwitch`** (tells you how close the next confusion swap is; eligible once it reaches 5).\n\nThe `turnsSinceLastSwitch` was also deduplicated into a local variable reused in both `debug.confusion` and `confusionMetrics.turnsSinceLastConfusionSwitch`.\n\nAll existing top-level fields (`currentModel`, `lastImplementationModel`, `lastReviewModel`, `lastDecision`, `confusionMetrics`) are preserved. SKILL.md updated to document the changed contract."
            },
            "type": "function",
            "toolTitle": "Task complete",
            "intentionSummary": "Enhanced `/model_router_status` output with two targeted ..."
          }
        ],
        "interactionId": "70cd13e4-10e0-4db3-8b4b-21174c1131bb",
        "outputTokens": 559
      }
    },

Here's a summary of what was done:\n\n**`extension.mjs` changes:**\n- Added `modelDiverged` — the most actionable field: instantly shows whether the router has swapped away from the implementation model (due to confusion or review diversity)\n- Nested confusion counters under `confusion.{isLooping, errorCount, repeatedPatternCount, turnsSinceLastSwitch}` — groups related signals and adds the previously-missing `turnsSinceLastSwitch` so you can see how close the next swap is (eligible at 5)\n- Extracted `activeModel` and `turnsSinceLastSwitch` as local variables to avoid recomputing them and to deduplicate the value in `confusionMetrics.turnsSinceLastConfusionSwitch`\n\n**`SKILL.md` update:** Docs updated to reflect the new `confusion.*` nested fields and the new `modelDiverged` field, since these materially change the visible status contract.\n\nAll existing fields (`currentModel`, `lastImplementationModel`, `lastReviewModel`, `lastDecision`, `confusionMetrics`) are preserved unchanged.\nEnhanced `/model_router_status` output with two targeted additions to the `debug` section:\n\n1. **`modelDiverged`** — boolean that's `true` when the active model differs from the last implementation model (e.g., after a confusion swap or review-diversity switch). This is the most actionable signal for debugging unexpected model switches.\n\n2. **Nested `confusion` object** replacing the flat counters — groups `isLooping`, `errorCount`, `repeatedPatternCount`, and the new **`turnsSinceLastSwitch`** (tells you how close the next confusion swap is; eligible once it reaches 5).\n\nThe `turnsSinceLastSwitch` was also deduplicated into a local variable reused in both `debug.confusion` and `confusionMetrics.turnsSinceLastConfusionSwitch`.\n\nAll existing top-level fields (`currentModel`, `lastImplementationModel`, `lastReviewModel`, `lastDecision`, `confusionMetrics`) are preserved.
